title: frailea cataphracta
intro: Frailea cataphracta, also known as the "ball cactus", is a small, spherical cactus species native to Brazil. It is prized for its small size, vibrant yellow flowers, and potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin infections, and digestive problems, providing benefits like promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and alleviating symptoms of fever and pain. Frailea cataphracta is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are employed to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
